reproduction_steps: Search requirements for "Attack Surface Map" section
evidence: No Attack Surface Map section found in requirements.
findings.impact: Without an Attack Surface Map, ownership/IDOR coverage cannot be verified.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an Attack Surface Map section to requirements (analyst).

reproduction_steps: Search spec-agent-execution-telemetry-bridge.md for SEC-SBD-07 evidence or N/A rationale
evidence: Surface "storage" present but no SEC-SBD-07 evidence or N/A rationale found in spec.
findings.impact: Control SEC-SBD-07 is required for surface "storage" but its evidence is missing.
findings.suggested_fix: Add evidence (or explicit N/A rationale) for SEC-SBD-07 in spec-agent-execution-telemetry-bridge.md.

findings.title: Retention is neither invoked automatically nor applied to nonterminal abandoned runs
findings.hypothesis: Repeated queued, running, correcting or paused runs retain events indefinitely and bypass the intended retention control.
preconditions: Executions are repeatedly paused, abandoned or crash before a terminal state
reproduction_steps: Insert an old paused run with events
Search callers and observe no production invocation of pruneExecutionTelemetry
evidence: src/runtime-store.js pruneExecutionTelemetry() deletes only passed, failed and cancelled runs
No production caller of pruneExecutionTelemetry is present in the reviewed diff
findings.impact: An attacker-controlled or malfunctioning pipeline can cause unbounded SQLite growth over many runs even when per-run output limits work.
findings.suggested_fix: Invoke bounded pruning on open/dispatch or via a documented maintenance command; expire stale nonterminal runs after reconciling them to an abandoned terminal state; add total database/run-count quotas and indexes/tests for pruning batches.
